item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has a very compelling setup, but I was waiting for a deeper narrative-some kind of reasoning behind the behavioral changes. Without this, I'm left to make my own with what little we're given. Is it that the jewelry contains some essence of the woman in the photo, which leads to the dissociation? The interview with the afflicted doesn't seem to support that-it points to something more alien, something that views any human form as wrong.

Also concur that the autopsy results, while technically extensive, didn't seem to add much beyond clarifying that the individual wasn't a victim of a homicide. That could have been done in a sentence or two.

Cut out the autopsy and work on the narrative. You don't have to give us everything wrapped up in a bow, but I think this is interesting enough that if you were to re-work it with more focus on the story and less on the effects, it could be quite successful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's competently written, but the effect of the item just seems like "crazy to death" played too straight to be interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor typo in the audio transcript, near the end, should probably say "stroll past" rather than "passed" because it's an adverb acting on "stroll."

Otherwise its pretty well written, a little heavy on the body horror for my tastes though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with Jcanti, the jewelry without any effects leave a good sense of wonder/mystery.

Personally, the only problem I had was with seeing that SCP-3580-a through e weren't capitalized (i.e. "SCP-3580-A" instead of "SCP-3580-a"), but that's more of a personal thing.

+1

This article seems to be real hit or miss.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it! It's an interesting concept with room for expansion and/or speculation with the other 3 pieces of jewelry with no observed affect. Maybe if all 5 pieces of jewelry are worn it cures the psychosis, maybe they worsen it? It really makes me think. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I was worried about the amount of detail but wanted to maintain the look of an authentic post-morterm report. I'll be sure to trim that as soon as I can. I've also replaced the locket photograph with one that is better sourced. I appreciate the warning and will be sure to avoid Pinterest in the future. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
well. It's competently done, I'll give you that; I might go so far as to say you've included too much technical detail. Remember, this is supposed to be an overview. If you're reading through the files to understand what this thing does because you've been assigned to it, you don't need the mass of a victim's lungs.

Thank you for sourcing the images properly, although please don't take stuff directly from Pinterest. The wedding pic is from 1900 so it's fine, the locket pic is almost certainly not fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi everyone! This is my first SCP, so please ream into me with everything you've got! Thank you all for your time!

Locket Source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Pendant_LACMA_AC1996.168.1.jpg
Bride and Groom Source: https://www.flickr.com/photos/okinawa-soba/3492939590/in/set-72157616817817339
